title: Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) has introduced SB 1042, to strengthen public safety by including human trafficking in the lists of crimes that are defined as serious and viol
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: An estimated 40 million people globally have been forced into modern slavery through human trafficking over the last decade.
Human trafficking is one of the world's fastest growing and most lucrative crimes, generating around $150 billion each year.
Victims of human trafficking often suffer from long-term physical and psychological trauma.
The American Academy of Pediatrics has recognized the severe trauma that human trafficking victims experience.
Human traffickers exploit individuals through violence and manipulation, leading to lives of modern-day slavery.
California ranks number one in the nation for the number of human trafficking cases reported to the National Human Trafficking Hotline.
Law enforcement agencies have dedicated special task forces to combat human trafficking in California.
Stronger consequences are required to hold criminals accountable for human trafficking.
The introduction of SB 1042 would classify human trafficking as a strike under California's Three Strikes law.
There is a need for penalties for repeat offenders of human trafficking.
The legislation aims to prevent traffickers from receiving early parole.
